name: Tech Tree
description: Tech Tree is a planning mechanic that governs territory, information, unit roles, resources, or long-horizon decisions.
design_notes: Tech Tree should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For strategy mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use tech tree as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect tech tree to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are winning through planning, information, and tradeoffs.

parameters.name: decision_horizon
parameters.description: Number of future steps the mechanic asks players to plan around.

parameters.name: information_radius
parameters.description: Area of map or state affected by information rules.

parameters.name: resource_pressure
parameters.description: How strongly the mechanic pushes opportunity cost.

edge_cases: Tech Tree is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Tech Tree overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Tech Tree runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Tech Tree state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Tech Tree plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Tech Tree produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Tech Tree strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Tech Tree becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Tech Tree with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Tech Tree as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Tech Tree with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Tech Tree as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
